name: Ravenquay
description: River delta city. Dawn Blades home base. 1 day sail south of Trevash, 3 days sail south of Khemrise.

districts.name: Docks Ward
districts.description: Working harbor, rough, practical. Smells of fish and tar. Criminal element present.

districts.name: Merchant Quarter
districts.description: More respectable. Established businesses, better kept streets.

districts.name: River Quarter
districts.description: Sits on the significant river running through the city. River trade important.

districts.name: Trade District
districts.description: Established commercial area. Guard HQ adjacent.

districts.name: Old Quarter
districts.description: Older part of city, established. No specific locations pinned yet.

districts.name: Academic Quarter
districts.description: Implied by Cartographer's Guild presence. Not fully established.

locations.name: Revivify
locations.description: Former Kreel residence, deeded to Order of Solmir. Dawn Blades refuge. Formerly upper-class lodging, thoroughly ransacked, repaired without upper-class talent — visible stitching on cushions, mismatched paint, some torn wallpaper. Clean, warm, charming, carries its history.
locations.notes: Marcus managing. Miren and Dav assisting.

locations.name: Loan Dragon
locations.description: Reformed loan shark operation.
locations.notes: 70/30 split favoring Dawn Blades. Approximately 25-30g/month income.

locations.name: Gilt Rose
locations.description: Helena's establishment. Discreet, well-appointed, charges enough to keep out trouble. Information brokering center. Regular clientele from upper social layer.

locations.name: Shrike's Perch
locations.description: Neutral ground tavern with private Rookery meeting rooms. Near the Gilt Rose.

locations.name: Broken Compass Tavern
locations.description: Siv's regular haunt.

locations.name: Calla Denn's Workshop
locations.description: Leatherworker. Needle and hide sign above door. Smells of leather, oil, and something harder to place underneath.

locations.name: Torvald Ironwood's Workshop
locations.description: Weaponsmith.

locations.name: Overflow Warehouse
locations.description: Wooden warehouse, medium sized, had a small stone basement with cells in it that is magically reinforced to be waterproofed, necessary for the water table.
locations.notes: Long Arm Trading property.

locations.name: Guard HQ
locations.description: Captain Crane's domain. Holding cells inside.
locations.notes: Duty sergeant recognizes Vaz on sight.

locations.name: The Arena
locations.description: Copper, Silver, and Gold tiers. Morning sessions start eighth bell. Betting available.
locations.notes: Dawn Blades registered a handful of teams.

locations.name: Morrow and Selt
locations.description: Chandler's shop. Casvath coastal relay access point.
locations.notes: Ask for Morrow, say 'grey tide is running'. Messages sent within the hour along coastal road north.

locations.name: Wayward Warehouse
locations.description: Legitimate mercenary job board.
locations.notes: Erasmus probably checks here.

locations.name: Black Anchor Tavern
locations.description: Pretty rough and tumble place
locations.notes: Disrupted after Dren's death.

locations.name: Cartographer's Guild
locations.description: Run by Finwick Gearspring, elderly gnome. Exploration and ruins work.

locations.name: Harrow House Inn
locations.description: Cut above typical inn. Quiet. Three streets from the Gilt Rose.
locations.notes: Ossivane Relk stayed here before departing for Argentveil.

flair: A river delta city where commerce and underworld politics are inseparable from each other and from the docks. People arrive here looking for a fresh start or a fast deal — often the same thing. Busy, layered, and never fully asleep.

name: Khemrise
description: Major coastal city, vineyards, pivot point where coast turns north-south. 2 days sail north of Trevash, 3 days sail north of Ravenquay.
climate: Temperate maritime. Fog burns off by midmorning every day without exception — locals treat this as a moral certainty.
notes: Harvest festival approximately 12 days out from current story position.

features.name: The Tidebell
features.description: Massive bronze bell at harbor entrance, rung at high tide since founding. Everyone sets their day by it.

districts.name: Harbor District
districts.description: Loud, practical, international. Ships from four regions. Street food that doesn't exist anywhere else. Fish market operating continuously for two hundred years. Sailors' taverns with honest music most nights.

districts.name: Merchant Quarter
districts.description: Where money shows itself. Tailors, jewelers, antiquities, spice merchants. Establishments that don't need signs because their clientele already knows where they are.

districts.name: Old Quarter
districts.description: Pre-city construction. Narrow streets, buildings lean toward each other overhead. Artisans, bookbinders, glassblower known regionally, small theaters with rotating productions, century-old bathhouse.

districts.name: Northern Market
districts.description: Commercial district. Spice imports, warehouse operations.

districts.name: Vineyard Road
districts.description: Connects city to hillside estates. Market stalls on festival days.

locations.name: Vineyard Estate
locations.description: On a vineyard hill. Private road connecting to coastal route, bypasses main harbor fees. Acquired from Ostwick family for 6,000g.

areas.name: Main House
areas.notes: Kitchen where Vaz had morning tea. Fireplace Petra talks to. Study off east corridor used as Glicken's temporary detention room. Desk where Tova works.

areas.name: East Outbuilding
areas.description: Converted for refugee accommodation. Clean and dry. Converting long term for wine aging.

areas.name: Stable
areas.notes: Where Fennick settled during estate gathering.

areas.name: Courtyard
areas.description: Central gathering space. Trestle tables for mass feeding. Mounting block near stable.
areas.notes: Where Vaz addressed crowd of 23 freed people.

areas.name: Vineyard Rows
areas.description: Morning light cuts long shadows between vines. Views to coast from eastern edge on clear morning.

areas.name: Perimeter Wall
areas.notes: Jaygar installed four clockwork traffic-detection devices at corners and gates.

areas.name: Estate Road
areas.description: Curves up through vineyard rows from main road below.

locations.name: Harbormaster Building
locations.description: Solid two-story stone, official signage. Ground floor clerk window with queue. Exterior stairs to upper office. Curtained upper window, heavy door.
locations.notes: Harbor watch active on all three Dawn Blades ships.

locations.name: Copper Wheel
locations.description: Well-kept tavern. Sign freshly painted, windows clean. Better maintained than surroundings suggest it should be.
locations.notes: Used to be Toreval Caine's place

locations.name: Legend's Clinic
locations.description: Coppersalt Lane — narrow street that doesn't appear on official maps. Fish smell, old rope. Blue shutter, grey door, no sign. Everyone knows it, nobody talks about it.
locations.notes: Removes collars.

locations.name: Salt Hook Tavern
locations.description: Harbor area. Bren the proprietor, no questions asked.
locations.notes: Vaz has room paid week upfront. Two keys to bank strongbox held by Vaz.

locations.name: Merchant's Bank of Khemrise
locations.description: Solid stone, brass fittings, cool interior, wood paneled. Long counter with clerk. Strongbox room private, small, unremarkable.
locations.notes: Dawn Blades strongbox registered under assumed name, Salt Hook address. Contains: negotiable notes (~39,000g).

locations.name: Drev's Office
locations.description: Thorngate Row. Ground floor warehouse, office above.
locations.notes: Went dark. Unoccupied now.

locations.name: Harwick's House
locations.description: Respectable merchant district. Neither wealthy enough to attract attention nor poor enough to suggest trouble. Front room carefully tidy.
locations.notes: Relay network documents on table.

locations.name: Corryn's Mast
locations.description: Sailmaker.
locations.notes: Three sets Dawn Blades flags made here.

locations.name: Rosten Alurath's Antiquities Shop
locations.description: Antiquities dealer.
locations.notes: Worth a visit.

flair: A city that considers itself a cultural pivot point because it sits at a geographical one. The money here is old. The wine is genuinely good. Smaller than many cities but with a lot of money moving through.

name: Trevash
description: Small coastal port, halfway between Ravenquay and Khemrise by sea. Rougher weather than Khemrise, more wind. Working port that doesn't attract leisure.

locations.name: Trevash Harbor
locations.description: Fisherman's moorings.
locations.notes: Aldric and Pell tie up at fisherman's moorings.

locations.name: Broken Tooth Cove
locations.description: 2 miles north of Trevash. Rock formation looks like a broken tooth from the water. Deep enough for a ship. Completely hidden from road.
locations.notes: Only Aldric and Pell know it. Offered to Dawn Blades as hidden anchorage asset.

locations.name: Waystation (Northern Road)
locations.description: On the road north of Trevash.
locations.notes: Wanted poster for Vaz posted here — 400g reward, Meridian Trading House contact. Road guard encounter happened here.

flair: Just a stop between bigger places — and that's exactly what gives it value. Everyone passing through has somewhere to be. Good for picking up information, bad for drawing attention to yourself.

name: Millhaven
description: Mining town, 4 days north of Ravenquay inland. Significantly colder, real winters. Single road in and out, good natural sight lines from approaches.
notes: Something in the deep tunnels connected to Kerra's patron three years ago. Kerra has tactical intelligence from a contract two years ago — approach roads, mine operation, and sight lines mapped in her memory.
flair: A mining town that works hard and asks few questions. The mine gives it purpose, the taverns give it character, and the dust never quite washes off anyone who lives here long.

name: Brennswick
description: Farming village, 30 miles east of Khemrise. Half day travel on good roads. Continental climate — hot summers, cold winters, good growing season between. Flat farmland before terrain rises toward mountains.

locations.name: Petyr Ostwick's Farm
locations.description: Ostwick family farm.
locations.notes: Dawn Blades acquired the Khemrise vineyard estate from this family. Petyr based here.

flair: Quiet, agricultural, and surprisingly difficult to forget.

name: Argentveil
description: Southernmost city on the western coast. Warmest and driest. Mediterranean feel — olive trees, stone architecture built for heat. Most cosmopolitan city in the region. 4-5 days sail east of Ravenquay.
notes: Chartered Investigators Act 15 years old, functioning, respected — model for Ravenquay charter. Beyond just being mediterranean style, Argentveil is also very magic-heavy. Dragonborn make up 40% of the population, many elves have found homes here along with plenty of others. Dwarves and orc-kin are now super rare. Various teiflings can be seen from time to time as well.  There are tall spires unlike any other city and a few districts have an abundance of wealth while others fill in the middle classes and the struggling. The docks are the safest in Kaemahz, crime finds its place elsewhere. Orvath lies a 6 day journed north/northeast over land into the drier climes, a fairly mild desert region.

locations.name: Argentveil City Council
locations.description: Sitting members include Galealzre.

locations.name: Watchtower Island
locations.notes: Small island approximately two hundred yards across at widest point. Ancient stone watchtower at center, highest point on the island. One natural landing spot on the south face, cliff or rock everywhere else. Home to a selkie community with historical and legal claim to the island predating Argentveil's establishment. Claim formalized through Dawn Blades intervention and Galealzre's council work, first legal framework established for aquatic species land rights in Fynesmarch. Selkies maintain the island and the watchtower. Strange lights reported from the tower periodically — confirmed as selkie practice rather than threat. Sael knows the approach well.

locations.name: The Measured Word
locations.notes: Behind the transmutation spire. Run by a retired wizard who makes approximately half his own stock. Worth visiting for unusual or crafted magical supplies.

locations.name: The Glass Sipper
locations.description: Next door to a glassblower, all their pots and cups are hand-blown and unique. Some of them supposedly make the tea taste different and you never know what you'll get. The glassblower refuses to make anything he finds ugly, so it has a stamp of his personality inside as well.

locations.name: The Fork & Roger
locations.description: Harbor area, good ale, adequate food, good place to keep an eye on the harbor.

locations.name: Linden Street Antiquities Market
locations.description: Where Ossivane Relk operates.

locations.name: Crestline & Vane
locations.ownership: rented — Dawn Blades occupy upper floor
locations.notes: Trading house on main floor, unaffiliated. Dawn Blades rent 3 rooms upstairs. Merchant district location gives good cover and access. Handblown glass vase in the center of the commonroom table, won by Kerra in the halfweek festival games.

locations.name: Torvaine's Steel
locations.notes: By referral only

locations.name: Forged & Found
locations.notes: Red awning

locations.name: DUVRAK
locations.notes: referral only

locations.name: The Folded Page
locations.description: Harbor end of the merchant quarter, Argentveil. Older than its neighbors, single room, shelves floor to ceiling on every wall. Stock is curated rather than comprehensive — nothing arrives here accidentally. The owner is an elderly man of indeterminate age.
locations.notes: Vaz has an open account of sorts — proprietor will contact when something relevant arrives.

flair: The warmest, wealthiest, most cosmopolitan city in Fynesmarch. People come here to be seen, to spend money, and to reinvent themselves. Fashion and ambition move faster here than anywhere else on the coast. Every magic school has an academy here and magical goods and users are commonplace. Very modern and very large.

name: Valdmere
description: Frontier city on the western coast tip. Coast faces south, northern and western coasts nearby. Wilderness and dense forest to the east. Seven day sail from Khemrise. Built around the Vald-mere, a shallow inland lake fed by winter runoff with a natural outlet to the coast.
climate: Temperate. Mild winters, rich fishing, well-watered farmland from seasonal runoff. Natural disasters uncommon. Food production exceptional.

history.first_settlement: A city existed here centuries ago, founded by people fleeing governmental persecution seeking freedom. Grew too dangerous internally, triggered mass exodus, fully abandoned.
history.second_settlement: Roughly 100 years ago a legend circulated about an artifact or font of immense power at this location. Two wealthy families funded expeditions, found nothing — the legend is almost certainly false — but built infrastructure in the process and the settlement stuck.
history.current_boom: Population exploded in the last five years. Driven by word of opportunity — rich fishing, abundant timber, viable mining and quarrying, fertile farmland, internal trade diversity. Everyone is an outsider so no one is. The gossip of opportunity became self-fulfilling.

government: None formal. Power is distributed between four major families, Clan Tetwhistle, criminal organizations, and individual dock owners. The families maintain rough balance — a power imbalance would be catastrophic for all of them and they know it. Disputes are resolved by strength, negotiation, or occasionally the implicit threat of either.
layout: Organic and chaotic. No planned quarters or districts. A natural main street of several blocks has emerged where foot traffic concentrates. Everything else is irregular blocks, opportunistic construction, and streets that exist because people kept walking the same path. Rapid expansion means buildings are beginning to appear outside the gate area.
demographics: Highly diverse. Every major race represented. No dominant demographic — the frontier nature attracted anyone willing to make the crossing from more established cities. Notable exception: halfling and gnome populations are significantly above continental average at approximately 25% and 15% respectively. Both races arrived early in the second settlement and established neighborhoods before the boom. Several distinct districts exist where architecture is built to halfling and gnome scale — full neighborhoods of half-sized houses, appropriately proportioned shops and public spaces. These neighborhoods are among the most established and well-organized in the city.

major_families.name: Lithcai'nael
major_families.origin: Unknown — predates written records of the family
major_families.wealth_source: Dragon slaying centuries ago. Hatched and raised two brass dragons, one killed in battle, one escaped into the wilds and has not been seen since. Converted draconic wealth into land, ownership, and taxation.
major_families.valdmere_presence: Largest district in the city. Residents pay a tax to the family. Militaristic, professionally trained forces. Not numerous but exceptionally capable.
major_families.appearance: All family armaments and crests feature polished brass and draconic motifs.
major_families.notes: Abandoned their original family name so long ago it has been forgotten by all but a few. Renamed themselves Lithcai'nael — roughly 'owners of the sky's wings.' The escaped brass dragon is a permanent liability — brass dragons are famously talkative and it carries centuries of family secrets.

major_families.name: Gaermuion
major_families.origin: Far east of the continent — exact location TBD pending continent map
major_families.wealth_source: Master alchemists and potion-makers. One of the oldest and most sophisticated potion-making lineages known. Their craft predates and exceeds anything available on the western coast.
major_families.valdmere_presence: Control most mining and quarrying operations — not because they are miners but because raw materials feed their actual work. Maintain a private laboratory compound in or near the city. Discreet but not hidden.
major_families.notes: Small family. Precise and methodical. Not interested in political theater. Their compound is quietly one of the most valuable facilities on this side of the continent. What they produce cannot be replicated elsewhere.

major_families.name: Wyllard
major_families.origin: First family to return to Valdmere in the second settlement
major_families.wealth_source: Infrastructure and construction. Built Valdmere's physical foundations and have been present at the founding of every significant enterprise since.
major_families.valdmere_presence: Most connected family in the city. Not the wealthiest or most powerful in any single domain but intermarried with half the merchant class and present in everything. Anyone wanting to expand their holdings needs Wyllard knowhow.
major_families.notes: Large, sprawling family. Pragmatic rather than cruel. Don't take the moral high ground often but understand that burning bridges costs more than it gains. Know where the foundations are weak on every building in the city, literally and figuratively. Hired by Clan Tetwhistle during canal construction — relationship ongoing.

major_families.name: Bostrom
major_families.origin: Former official clothiers and textile suppliers to pre-merchant-revolution royalty across the continent
major_families.wealth_source: Premium textile trade to monarchies. Helped overthrow those same monarchies alongside merchant guilds, destroying their own primary market in the process. Made increasingly bad decisions afterward and ran out of capital without recovering.
major_families.valdmere_presence: The family is effectively gone as a power. The name carries the ghost of former influence. Descendants are scattered across the social spectrum — some in Valdmere, some elsewhere, some clinging to the name, some having abandoned it.
major_families.notes: The family's actual skill was never textiles — it was reading political winds and positioning accordingly. That skill still exists in some descendants and is worth considerably more in Valdmere than any craft. A Bostrom who understands this is an interesting person.

emerging_powers.name: Clan Tetwhistle
emerging_powers.race: Mixed — coalition of three to four small human families
emerging_powers.origin: Ravenquay shipbuilders. Arrived approximately 50 years ago, roughly 25 heads.
emerging_powers.wealth_source: Claimed vast swampy, mosquito-ridden, non-arable land no one else wanted. Used a 300 gold loan from Ravenquay creditors against existing collateral and reputation, hired labor including Wyllard contractors, and built a canal connecting the Vald-mere to the coast. Now operate the canal toll and the lake moorings — calmer and safer than coastal docks.
emerging_powers.valdmere_presence: Control the canal and lake moorings. Strategically critical infrastructure. The swamp land no one wanted is now some of the most valuable real estate in the city.
emerging_powers.notes: Clan rather than family — the coalition identity is deliberate and maintained. Still have strong Ravenquay connections giving them advance knowledge of maritime arrivals. Internal governance is their biggest challenge as the clan has grown. The major families allowed their land claim because swamp wasn't worth fighting over — that calculation has not aged well for the older families. Dawn Blades are Ravenquay-based — relationship with Tetwhistle worth developing.

power_structures.criminal_organizations: Several gangs operate in the gaps between family territories. The line between criminal organization and de facto neighborhood authority is deliberately blurry. Some provide genuine services — dispute resolution, protection for those who can't afford family patronage. Some are predatory. All have working relationships with at least one major family.

locations.name: The Velvet Flair
locations.description: Mid-range inn presenting slightly above its surroundings. Clean, intentional, the ambition of it visible in small details. Clientele tends toward travelers with means and discretion.
locations.notes: Tierna Ashborne resident, room four. Previously where Harven Dross stayed.

locations.name: Tallow and Tar
locations.description: Better than it sounds. Run by a woman who knows everything worth knowing about Valdmere and a fair amount worth knowing about everything else. Does not share information directly — tells you what she thinks you need when she thinks you need it.
locations.notes: Recommended by Osmer. Tell her Osmer sent you.

locations.name: Temple of Voryn
locations.description: Modest footprint, single story with partial loft. Living vines cover the eastern face, flowering ahead of season. Raw timber doorframe with carved patterns that almost resolve into animals. Interior blurs the boundary between inside and outside — open southern wall section, living trees incorporated into corners, partial open roof. Has changed hands multiple times — cartographer's guild, merchant collective, current Voryn congregation.
locations.notes: Contains an ancient teleportation circle beneath the main hall flagstones. Current Keeper is aware of it. Dawn Blades have negotiated access. Two former enemy operatives now working temple floor restoration as penance. Circle activation planned.

locations.name: City Gate
locations.description: More than a gate — an ecosystem. New customs checkpoint, raw timber construction. Arrivals, labor for hire, information brokers, people waiting for someone who hasn't come yet. Buildings spreading outside the gate as the city expands faster than its own boundaries.
locations.notes: Three separate groups observed watching the gate on party's arrival day. Registration requirement for non-humans above certain size threshold — currently under review following Lenn Ashford's intervention.

locations.name: The Vald-mere
locations.description: The shallow lake the city is named for. Fed by winter runoff, natural outlet to the coast. Calmer and safer than coastal waters. Fishing boats, Tetwhistle canal entrance, lake moorings.
locations.notes: The original settlement grew around the lake rather than the coast. The lake came first — the city grew outward from it.

locations.name: Tetwhistle Canal and Lake Moorings
locations.description: Canal connecting the Vald-mere to the coast. Built 50 years ago by Clan Tetwhistle on land no one else wanted. Toll-operated. Lake moorings calmer and safer than coastal docks. Most serious maritime traders prefer it.
locations.notes: The swamp land surrounding the canal operation is Tetwhistle's claim and is now some of the most strategically valuable real estate in the city.

locations.name: Lithcai'nael District
locations.description: Largest single district in the city. You know when you've crossed into it — brass and draconic motifs on everything the family owns. Residents pay a family tax. Well-maintained relative to the rest of Valdmere.
locations.notes: Professionally trained security force. Not numerous but exceptionally capable.

locations.name: Gaermuion Compound
locations.description: Private laboratory and family residence. Not a shop or public-facing business. Discreet but not hidden.
locations.notes: Quietly one of the most valuable facilities on this side of the continent. Access is not casually granted.

locations.name: Wyllard Holdings
locations.description: Less a defined district than a presence throughout the city — construction yards, material depots, offices embedded in multiple neighborhoods.
locations.notes: The family is everywhere without appearing to dominate anywhere specifically.

locations.name: Coastal Docks
locations.description: Privately owned docks, no harbor authority. Port fees set by individual dock owners. Information about arrivals fragmented and purchasable.
locations.notes: Smuggling is trivially easy. No unified oversight.

locations.name: Low Roof Districts
locations.description: Several neighborhoods built entirely to halfling and gnome scale. Half-sized houses, appropriately proportioned streets and shops. Among the oldest and best-established neighborhoods in Valdmere — both races arrived early in the second settlement before the boom and claimed their ground accordingly.
locations.notes: Pirra will find these neighborhoods immediately. Gaermuion family presence likely strongest here given gnome population density.

locations.name: Unnamed Tea Shop
locations.description: Unnamed establishment with no sign. Three tables, six chairs, shelves floor to ceiling with jars, bundles and unidentifiable containers. The smell is extraordinary and shifts depending on where you're standing. Scaled for halfling and gnome clientele.
locations.notes: Run by Wrellia, halfling woman of indeterminate age. Makes compounds and drinks that address what people actually need rather than what they ask for. Her sister lives near the Bluff House. Said the party would come back with the certainty of a fact rather than an invitation. Found by following the smell through a slightly open door.

flair: A frontier port at the edge of the settled coast. Far enough from the center that it makes its own rules and gets away with it. Ships stop here that aren't going anywhere else. Currently booming and ungoverned.

name: Cindermarch
description: Fortress city, northeast of Khemrise. On the Fol border. 3 days northeast of Khemrise, 1 day beyond Millhaven, 4-5 days ride from Ravenquay. Positioned between two rising mountain ranges, right where the weather changes, with Inkaba — an active volcano — visible to the east on clear days. Snow to the north, clear to the south most of the year. Conflict with Fol has ended; the fortress remains and the city has grown around it.

locations.name: Cindermarch Fortress
locations.description: The fortress proper.
locations.notes: Salla Kern is Master-at-Arms. Where Kerra was enslaved before transfer to Khemrise estate. Caravan escort destination early campaign. Salla Kern sent job offer regarding Kerra — follow-up pending.

flair: A city built to hold a line that no longer needs holding, until it does again. The military architecture is still there. The martial culture is still there. But the walls haven't been seriously threatened in years, and the fortress-identity and city-identity pull against each other in everything from local politics to tavern conversation.

name: Orvath
description: Free city built around a desert oasis — the only reliable water source in the region, which is why the city exists where it does. Streets are wide and the architecture reflects both desert practicality and elven aesthetic sensibility. Primarily elven population, Mongolian-inspired culture. The banking district is the reason most outsiders make the journey.
notes: Question-free banking hub — no extradition, anonymous deposits. 6 days north/northeast of Argentveil overland.

locations.name: Orvath Banking Institution
locations.description: Anonymous deposits, no personal appearance required for withdrawal.

flair: One city, one unique function, elevated to a cultural institution. The banking culture has shaped everything — the architecture favors discretion, the social norms reward neutrality, strangers are treated with professional courtesy that never tips into familiarity. Elven-Mongolian cultural mix that keeps outsiders slightly off-balance in ways they cannot quite name.

name: Wilderness & Roads
description: Named wilderness locations and road features across Fynesmarch.

locations.name: Crossroads Rest
locations.description: Waystation approximately 2 days north of Ravenquay on the main road.

locations.name: Valley Ambush Site
locations.description: Natural funnel in highland terrain north of Ravenquay.
locations.notes: Where Malrik and Vess were intercepted by Dawn Blades. Both turned, sent north with false all-clear message.

locations.name: Coastal Road
locations.description: Runs between Ravenquay and Khemrise along the coast.
locations.notes: Casvath relay network operates along it. Harwick relay network being built alongside it with staging points.

locations.name: Verath Ohn
locations.description: Off the southern coast, in the water.

locations.name: Forest Trolls, roughly
locations.description: Northwest of Khemrise, several days journey.

locations.name: The Breathing Tomb
locations.location: Northern forest, several days northeast of Valdmere
locations.description: Underground complex predating Valdmere and likely all current Kaemahz civilization. Two chambers and a connecting corridor. Upper chamber is a tomb containing dozens of preserved figures of varied ages, hands cupped upward, names recorded on vaulted ceiling. Lower chamber is an archive — hundreds of sealed stone containers lining every wall, a stone bellows breathing mechanism in the center, and a continuous carved narrative covering the entire ceiling vault. Two suits of armor in dark unidentified metal stand at the archive entrance, depicted by name in the ceiling scene.
locations.access: Natural hollow in forest floor. Rotating stone mechanism opens from above after a two inch drop release. Can also be opened from below via lever. Doorman skeleton remains at interior lever.
locations.atmosphere: Bone dry, preserved. Breathing sound audible from surface when open. Air circulation maintained by deep stone bellows mechanism.
locations.notes: Script matches ridge complex ruins and scroll from disc box. Dhovari may be able to read it. Bring Tongues spell. Resealed via Mage Hand on departure. Armor suits appear to be named individuals from the civilization. Archive contents unknown. Second door in tomb chamber leads to corridor and archive — second lever operates it from tomb side. Corridor predates tomb chamber.

name: Tilawam
location: Eastern face of the Yetgrese crescent, slightly north of the main waterway gap, facing the open exterior sea
description: A low coastal city at the easternmost point of Yetgrese, where the old crater wall has fully eroded away. The land rises gradually inland — no dramatic topography. Built from rough-hewn uncut stone: irregular, natural-shaped, no two buildings quite alike. Thatched roofs coated annually in dark tar give the whole roofline a blackened character. Every building has one central chimney; seen from a distance the skyline is a forest of single chimneys at varying heights. Main thoroughfares are cobbled; side streets are mud, difficult after rain. Locals know which routes stay driest.
flair: Oral history storytelling circles/events, gritty underground fight scene, Hei'ugar market and fishing culture, Drevani punk aesthetic, Catrin merchant culture. Very little magic use or practitioners. Axebeak racing is the city's dominant spectator sport — held on a flat track on the outskirts, heavy gambling, loud crowds, and deeply embedded in daily culture. The Drevani are the most devoted fanbase and frequently the boldest bettors. The racing breed is a selectively bred lineage — noticeably smaller than the standard axebeak but significantly faster, lighter-boned, and bred for acceleration over short distances. Late summer the city repaints simultaneously — faded orange and turquoise go suddenly vivid across every street at once, a collective event locals treat with evident seriousness. The Drevani neighbourhood has no gate, no sign, no hard line — you notice you are in it because the buildings run slightly smaller, the decorations run louder, and there is more leather, dye, and piercing per block than anywhere else in the city.
climate: unknown — eastern exterior coast, sheltered partially by the crescent geography

government.origin: Approximately 100 years ago a monarchy collapsed following civil unrest. Two factions emerged simultaneously: a conglomerate of aristocrats who seized power, and a union of working-class government officials (sheriffs, administrators, etc.) from across the region who declared their own right to govern. Both sides recognised that further conflict was worse than compromise. Neither acknowledged the other formally. Over a century of deals, arguments, partnerships, and evolution, the arrangement solidified into a functioning dual system.

magistrates.description: Seats filled by votes from the upper classes. Represent merchant, aristocratic, and wealthy interests.
magistrates.collective_power: Equal to the councillors despite lower numbers — individual magistrates carry significantly more weight per vote

councillors.description: Seats filled by votes from the lower classes. Represent working, craft, and common interests.

government.banking_role: Banks are responsible for truthfully reporting household value, which determines class designation and therefore voting eligibility. As a result the banking institutions are exceptionally sophisticated and nearly impenetrable — protected by both governments simultaneously. Attacking or corrupting the banks is the one act that would unite both governments instantly.
government.deadlock_mechanism: No law or decision can be made on a tie vote. There are no tiebreakers — the matter returns to discussion and comes back two weeks later. This prevents gaming the system through manufactured deadlock while ensuring genuinely contested decisions move slowly.
government.notes: The system is slow. It is also, by design, mostly not war. Both sides have the arrangement baked deeply enough into their institutions that dismantling it would require defeating the other side entirely — a calculation neither has found worthwhile in a century.

demographics: Tilawam's population is a genuine cosmopolitan mix unlike anything in the other continenets. Catrins form the majority at roughly half the city's population — the broad, ridge-jawed people most visitors associate with Yetgrese's exterior coast. Drevani make up around ten percent, small and fluffy and disproportionately visible in the dock district where their punk aesthetic and fearless energy make them impossible to miss. Hei'ugar account for another five percent, grey-undertoned and stocky, living mostly on the city's outskirts where they farm the surrounding land and fish the coastal waters with the quiet devotion of people for whom open sky is a kind of prayer. Humans are present but less than ten percent — traders mostly, short-term residents, the occasional person who came for a crossing and stayed. The remaining quarter or so of the population is a mix of elven subraces unique to the continent.

economy.notes: Trade port. Receives occasional crossings from Kaemahz — rare enough that the party's arrival is noteworthy. Maritime salvage claims processed through harbour authority with 3-5 day review period. Market district exists with standard trading functions. Banking institutions are exceptional by any standard. People do not say prices out loud, but rather write them down and pass it to the buyer. It's bad luck to say a price out loud.

harbour.description: Sheltered by two ancient stone breakwaters of pre-current-civilisation construction. Lower harbour distinct from merchant docks — Davan directed the party to the lower harbour specifically. Harbour authority present and functional. Current pattern between the breakwaters is non-obvious and not captured on Kaemahz charts.
harbour.notes: Harbour officer assigned to vessels involved in piracy incidents as standard procedure. Salvage vessels held pending 3-5 day review before sale or transfer permitted.

locations.name: Lower Harbour
locations.description: Distinct from the merchant docks. Davan directed the party here on arrival. Ancient breakwaters.
locations.notes: Current pattern between breakwaters is tricky — not on Kaemahz charts. Manageable with local knowledge.

locations.name: Harbour Authority Office
locations.description: Standard arrival processing, declaration forms, salvage claim registration.
locations.notes: Quiveth was based here as translator before being hired by the party.

locations.name: Bathhouse
locations.description: Located near the dockside establishment. Hot water, private rooms available. Well developed.
locations.notes: First stop for the party on arrival.

locations.name: Dockside Establishment
locations.description: Caters to dock workers and sailors. Unpretentious, good food, stays open late. Communal and welcoming of strangers. Games available, some unfamiliar to Kaemahz visitors.
locations.notes: Recommended by Quiveth. Party's first meal in Tilawam. Arm wrestling exists here universally.

locations.name: Market District
locations.description: Standard trading functions. Opens at dawn.
locations.notes: Quiveth will escort party here tomorrow for selling salvage cargo, scales, jaw material, and other goods.

locations.name: Repose
locations.district: Dock district, three streets back from lower harbour
locations.description: Quiet older stone building set back from the waterfront. Two stories with internal courtyard. No street signage. Common room with hearth on ground floor. Four rooms — two larger courtyard-facing, two smaller street-facing. Breakfast included, dinner available on request.
locations.notes: Recommended by Quiveth. Caters to traders and merchants rather than sailors.

locations.name: Sorreval and Sons
locations.description: Three generation trading house. Permanent frontage in merchant district. House mark above door in iron.
locations.notes: Formal trade partnership with Dawn Blades. Handles Tilawam-side storage, buyers, suppliers.

locations.name: Ssivanak's Workshop
locations.description: Upper district. Dark green door, no signage. Dense, organized interior. Back room houses large items.
locations.notes: Only magical item dealer in Tilawam.

locations.name: Brama Coinsmith's Office
locations.description: Upper district. Identified by copper door.

locations.name: Artificers Guild
locations.description: Craftsman district. Workshop-focused, primarily mechanical and alchemical disciplines. Four generation precision instrument family associated.
locations.notes: Gifted vibration deterrent device to party. Not enchantment-capable.

locations.name: Stillwater Harbour
locations.description: Natural deep water anchorage inside southern archipelago island cluster. Invisible from exterior approach. Channel entrance narrow with two turns. Iron chain across inner channel. Two buildings against interior rock face, one partially demolished. Existing dock infrastructure. Capacity for six ships.
locations.notes: Former Stillwater pirate base. Claimed by Dawn Blades. Eastern gap gated. Chain key held by Dawn Blades. Repair and development project underway.

notes: Language barrier significant — Common is not widely spoken.

name: Halvenmoren
description: Border town on the Fynesmarch side of Dunric Pass. Old enough to have two naming traditions visible in its architecture — the newer Fynesmarch quarter descriptive and practical, the older Torwald-origin buildings heavier, stonework that suggests someone's grandfather built to last. Sits at the mouth of the pass road. Most traffic through here is either going somewhere or coming from somewhere cold.
climate: Cooler than Argentveil, exposed to pass weather when the wind turns. Spring arrives late here.
notes: Primary gateway settlement for Dunric Pass.

locations.name: The Greyed Lintel
locations.description: Two-story stone inn, ground floor tavern. The lintel stone above the main door is noticeably lighter than the surrounding stonework — replaced after a fire three owners ago. Name stuck.
locations.notes: Primary gathering point for stranded merchants and locals alike. Louder than usual given the pass closure.

locations.name: Pass Road Gate
locations.description: Stone gatehouse at the north edge of town where the road narrows toward the pass. Maintained by two town guards of variable commitment.
locations.notes: Merchants have been stacking up here for eleven days. The mood is deteriorating.

locations.name: Jax's Place
locations.description: Narrow building wedged between a cooperage and a feed store. Original use unclear — too small for a proper shop, too cluttered for a house. Windows papered from inside with what looks like old maps. Completely stacked full with hand-written books that appear to be gibberish.
locations.notes: Jax has lived here four years. Locals treat him as harmless local color. The papered windows are deliberate — nobody can see in.

flair: A border town defined almost entirely by what it guards against. The Dunric Pass is the reason it exists; everything else about it is secondary to that geographic fact.

name: Filian's Keep
description: Capital of Torwald. A working fortress-city built on a defensible hill — practical and unadorned in the Torwald tradition. Stone streets, solid buildings, nothing wasted on decoration that doesn't also serve a purpose. The keep itself predates the city that grew around it.
flair: The capital of a culture that has decided it represents authentic tradition. Everything here is done the right way — which is also the old way. Impressive in the manner of things preserved with great care. Visitors often feel they are being judged against a standard they were never told about.

name: Feldtazur
description: Capital of Aranwald. The city visually layers 200 years of Solvaran architecture over the older Torwald-origin construction beneath — arched courtyards, geometric tilework, and repurposed minarets sit alongside the original Saxon-influenced stonework. The Solvaran additions are displayed as trophies, not erased as shame. Walking through Feldtazur is a study in the prince's founding philosophy: we didn't just conquer the land, we conquered everything. Name coined by the prince — Anglo 'feld' + Persian-derived 'tazur'.
flair: A capital built to prove a philosophical point: that to conquer something is to own everything about it, including its beauty. Persian-Anglo aesthetic fusion that is layered, intentional, and never subtle. The architecture alone tells the kingdom's entire founding story.

name: Yshavar
description: Capital of the Solvale Empire. Built for imperial scale — wide boulevards, domed public buildings, formal gardens, grand architecture designed to house more people and more ambition than currently inhabit it. The bones of empire are still impressive; the city is well-maintained by habit. But it feels like a coat cut for a larger person. Population and commerce have contracted since the collapse; the grandeur remains, sitting in slightly too much space.
flair: An imperial capital still operating at imperial scale with half the people. The public buildings are maintained by habit. The art is extraordinary — the empire knew what it was doing at its height. The grandeur is genuine. The gap between the city's physical presence and its current population is felt everywhere, like a conversation where one person left the room and nobody mentioned it.

name: Drinaell
description: Coastal seafaring city and maritime hub of Birindal. Celtic/Nordic character — stone and timber construction built to last in sea weather, harbor infrastructure expanded and rebuilt over generations. The city smells of salt, tar, and woodsmoke. Ship captains are a social class unto themselves. Likely the primary naval power on Kaemahz.
flair: The dominant maritime force in Kaemahz, and it carries itself accordingly. Celtic/Nordic feel with a merchant edge rather than a raiding one — the harbor council governs, the sea provides, and outsiders are welcomed as long as they have something to offer. Everyone here has an opinion about ships.

name: Brakvunder
description: Dwarven mountain city carved into the northeastern range of Kaemahz. The mountain is the city — generations of expansion have pushed deeper and wider into the rock. Renowned for metalwork and stonecraft. Maintains a working trade partnership with Drinaell on the coast, supplying finished goods in exchange for sea access and external trade networks.
flair: A dwarven mountain city where craft is as close to religion as anything gets, and independence is non-negotiable. Sends finished goods down to Drinaell, takes what it wants from outside, and ignores the rest. Visitors are not unwelcome — just clearly secondary to the work.
